channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Guatemalan judge grants investigative journalist Zamora house arrest and his family celebrates
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — The family of a Guatemalan journalist on Saturday celebrated his transfer to house arrest following his jailing for more than two years amid his daily newspaper's anti-corruption investigations.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PEREZ D., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Two Texas cities represent the divide between those who vote and those who could, but often don't
item.description: Two neighboring Texas cities illustrate the divide in American politics between the diverse, potential electorate in the country and the actual electorate, which is heavily white and wealthier.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hawaiian kayaker, 17, treads water for 12 hours before rescue
item.description: "I was actually crying my guts off because he was okay,” the lifeguard who helped save the teen said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Minimum wage on the ballot in these 5 states
item.description: The federal minimum wage hasn't changed since 2009 but voters in several states will have the chance to raise hourly wages at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With brain injuries a growing problem, the US military tests how to protect troops from blasts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The blast shook the ground and its red flash of fire covered the doorway as U.S. special operations forces blew open a door during a recent training exercise.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing Virginia antique shop owner's family believes he was murdered after months without answers
item.description: The family of missing Virginia antique store owner Kenneth Turner believes he was led from his home in April and killed, though they are still working to find answers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia parents charged with child cruelty and false imprisonment of 4 teenagers
item.description: Two Georgia parents are facing charges over accusations of child cruelty and false imprisonment of their four teenagers who were living under conditions described as inhumane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belgrade's landmark Hotel Yugoslavia faces likely demolition, with many opposed
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Belgrade’s iconic Hotel Yugoslavia, once a symbol of progress in the former socialist state of Yugoslavia that broke apart in the 1990s and a favorite gathering place for local residents as well as world leaders, now stands in eerie silence awaiting its likely demolition.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Freedom of expression threatened more seriously in Gaza than in any recent conflict, UN expert says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Freedom of expression has been threatened more seriously in Gaza than in any recent conflict, with journalists targeted in the war-torn territory and Palestinian supporters targeted in many countries, a United Nations expert said Friday. Irene Khan, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony has a poignant vibe this year
item.description: The Rock & Roll Hall Fame induction ceremony on Saturday night promises to be starry, jamming — and bittersweet.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Before 19-year-old Davontez Johnson found himself in a preschool classroom at Dorothy I. Heights Elementary, he was a senior at a nearby high school who, like many students his age, was unsure of what he wanted to do with his life.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chumash people in California to co-steward marine sanctuary in historic partnership
item.description: For more than 10,000 years, Native Americans have been living along California’s central coast, an area of breathtaking beauty with stunning turquoise waters rich in biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews halt fast-moving brush fire that burned 2 Oakland homes, forced 500 evacuations
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fast-moving fire fed by strong winds burned two homes Friday and damaged several others in a hillside neighborhood in the city of Oakland, where hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate before more than 100 firefighters stopped the progress of the flames, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Texas 'junk science' law reason for temporary stay of execution
item.description: Vanessa Potkin of The Innocence Project says a subpoena from the Texas state legislature has saved Roberson’s life — for the moment.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Kentucky family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas troopers find 134 illegal immigrants near border, some from Iran, authorities say
item.description: Texas authorities on Thursday caught a large group of illegal immigrants near the southern border as the state steps up efforts to combat the flow of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury paves the way for 2 Live Crew to retake control of records that changed hip-hop
item.description: Miami's 2 Live Crew helped redraw the legal landscape around what hip-hop could be, pushing the boundaries of free speech and taste with their provocative and sexually explicit recordings that led to landmark court decisions protecting the rights of artists.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former MTV VJ Ananda Lewis reveals stage 4 breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The TV personality first revealed she was diagnosed with breast cancer years ago, but it has progressed and metastasized since.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Border Report Live: Illegal immigration drives up border violence
item.description: The head of the Border Patrol's El Paso Sector recently described the crime of assaulting a federal agent as "an affront to our principles of justice and the safety of our nation.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama executes man who killed 5 and asked to be put to death
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — Alabama executed a man Thursday who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals and asked to be put to death. Derrick Dearman, 36, was pronounced dead at 6:14 p.m. Thursday at Holman prison in southern Alabama.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris: Trump 'has no idea' about women's health care
item.description: “The man calls himself the father of IVF? I mean what does that even mean?” said Harris, referencing her rival’s comments on in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daughter claims mother's body 'misplaced' in Tennessee cemetery
item.description: "They have no idea where or how she was lost, but they do not know where she is," says Georleshia Minter.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma parents and teachers sue to stop top education official's classroom Bible mandate
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A group of Oklahoma parents of public school students, teachers and ministers filed a lawsuit Thursday seeking to stop the state's top education official from forcing schools to incorporate the Bible into lesson plans for students in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years but parents of an Independence second grader say she's already had her first crash.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, ABC 4

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds and now includes ready-to-eat meals sent to U.S. schools, restaurants and major retailers, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez brothers' family to push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison following the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Long lost sisters meet for the first time in person
item.description: Two long-lost sisters who met for the first time in person say their love will continue even after this life.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, Fox 5
